semanticCore: Concealment, chest, shelter, covering, or shield
quranicRelevance: Shelters/homes (Aknaan - 'wa ja'ala lakum min al-jibali aknanan'), covers over hearts (Akinmah - 'wa ja'alna 'ala quloobihim akinnatan an yafqahoohu'), and guarded/preserved (Maknoon - 'lu'lu'on maknoon').

keyDerivations.meaning: To conceal, hide, keep secret, or shelter
Veils, covers, shields, or barriers over hearts

figurativeOrRareUsages: أَكِنَّةً أَنْ يَفْقَهُوهُ: Veils/covers over their hearts lest they understand it (spiritual barrier to comprehension).
كأَمْثَالِ اللُّؤْلُؤِ المَكْنُونِ: Like the likeness of well-protected/preserved pearls (pure and untouched).
discardedNoise: Obscure details of camel shelter construction.
summaryEn: The root represents protection and veiling, depicting physical shelters (Aknaan), preserved assets (pearls), and the spiritual veils (Akinmah) of hearts.
summaryAr: يمثل هذا الجذر الوقاية والستر، واصفاً الكهوف والملاذات الجبلية الحامية (أكنان)، واللؤلؤ المصون (مكنون)، والحجاب المعنوي للقلب (أكنة).
rawText: فاعل مُسْتَكِنٌّ : see مُسْتَقِنٌ. كَانُونٌ A fire-place; a place in which fire is lighted. (S, K.) كُنَّةٌ : see طَنَفٌ. كِنَانَةٌ : see جَعْبَةٌ. كِنٌّ A place of retreat or concealment; such as a cave, and an excavated house or chamber: (Beyd, xvi. 83:) see بَرٌّ. === كِنٌّ The shelter of a wall: see دِفْءٌ. === A thing that serves for veiling, covering, or protecting. (S, * Msb, * K.) See ظِلٌّ, where I have thus rendered it. مَكْنُونُ الفَائِلِ : see a verse of El-Aashà in art. فيل.
